---
title: "Why ZK?"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status:** Stub. Full chapter coming soon.
:::

## The question

You can encrypt your database. You can use TLS. You can put data behind an
auth layer. **Why on earth do you need zero-knowledge proofs?**

## What this chapter will cover

- The three properties only ZK gives you: **privacy + verifiability + decentralization** simultaneously
- Why you can't get those three with traditional crypto (you can get any two)
- A side-by-side comparison: encrypted database vs Bitcoin UTXO vs Aleo records
- The "compliance without surveillance" pattern (foreshadowing the
  [Merkle trees](../02-cryptographic-primitives/02-merkle-trees.qmd) chapter)

## Interactive viz planned

A 3-axis comparison widget: pick two of {private, verifiable, decentralized}
and see which traditional system gives you that pair, and why no traditional
system gives you all three.

## Why this is chapter 1

Every other chapter in this book pre-supposes you understand *why* anyone would
use Aleo instead of Postgres + signatures. If you can answer that in one
sentence, you can skip ahead. If not, this chapter is the foundation.
